metadata.methodology: AI CapEx figures are based on company-disclosed AI-specific spending where available. Where not explicitly disclosed, estimates are derived from total CapEx, earnings call guidance ('vast majority', 'primarily'), and analyst reports. All figures in USD billions. Market cap figures as of March 2026. Companies with null aiCapex are private and do not disclose capital expenditure.

confidenceLegend.verified: Confirmed from 10-K filing, earnings release, or authoritative financial data site
confidenceLegend.estimated: Derived from analyst estimates, management commentary, or indirect calculation
confidenceLegend.unverified: Source not found or data is from unconfirmed press reports

companies.name: Amazon
companies.note: AWS infrastructure + custom AI chips (Trainium, Inferentia). 'Vast majority' of capex is AI/AWS.

companies.name: Alphabet
companies.note: Google Cloud + DeepMind + Gemini. ~60% of capex on servers (GPUs/TPUs), ~40% on data centers.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.note: Azure AI + OpenAI partnership. Official guidance: '$80B on AI-enabled data centers in FY2025'. Capex incl. finance leases.

companies.name: Meta
companies.note: LLaMA training + MTIA chips + Reality Labs AI. Capex 'primarily' for AI infrastructure. +80% YoY.

companies.name: TSMC
companies.note: AI chip fabrication capacity expansion (N3, N2 nodes). AI/HPC is primary driver. 2026 capex guided $56B.

companies.name: Samsung
companies.note: HBM memory production for AI + AI chip R&D. DS division capex KRW 47.5T (~$33B), heavily HBM/AI.

companies.name: ByteDance
companies.note: AI infrastructure for TikTok recommendation + LLM development. Nearly all capex is AI-related. Rising to ~$23B in 2026.

companies.name: Oracle
companies.note: OCI cloud + Stargate project (OpenAI). CapEx +209% YoY. 'More than tripled data center capex.'

companies.name: Intel
companies.note: Foundry buildout (Intel 18A) + Gaudi AI accelerators. Gross capex $17.7B, net ~$11.2B after subsidies.

companies.name: Tencent
companies.note: Hunyuan LLM + WeChat AI + cloud AI. CNY 79.2B capex, primarily AI infrastructure.

companies.name: Alibaba
companies.note: Alibaba Cloud AI + Tongyi Qianwen LLM. Announced CNY 380B ($52.4B) multi-year AI commitment.

companies.name: Nvidia
companies.note: AI GPU R&D + Blackwell production. Fabless model: own capex is small but enables $400B+ in customer AI spend. FY2026 = CY2025.

companies.name: Apple
companies.note: Apple Intelligence on-device AI + Private Cloud Compute. Uses 'hybrid model' (first-party + third-party). AI capex far smaller than peers.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.note: FSD training (Cortex: 50K H100 + 16K H200). ~$1B/quarter on AI infra. Dojo shut down Aug 2025.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.note: Custom AI ASICs (Google TPU, Meta MTIA) + AI networking. Fabless: capex minimal, AI investment is through $11B R&D.

companies.name: Baidu
companies.note: Ernie Bot LLM + Apollo Go autonomous driving. Revenue declining -3% YoY. ~55% of workforce is R&D.

companies.name: IBM
companies.note: watsonx platform + Granite models + AI consulting. AI investment primarily through $8.1B R&D, not capex.

companies.name: Dell Technologies
companies.note: AI server manufacturing (PowerEdge AI). AI server orders $10B+ backlog.

companies.name: Qualcomm
companies.note: On-device AI chips (Snapdragon X Elite) + AI modem R&D. Fabless: AI investment is through $9B R&D.

companies.name: Cisco
companies.note: AI networking infrastructure + Webex AI + security AI. AI investment through $9.3B R&D. Capex +35% YoY.

companies.name: HPE
companies.note: AI-optimized servers + GreenLake AI cloud. FY2024 data (FY ends Oct).

companies.name: ServiceNow
companies.note: Now Assist AI agents + workflow intelligence. Revenue +21% YoY.

companies.name: AMD
companies.note: MI300 AI accelerator R&D + Instinct GPU line. Fabless: AI investment through $8.1B R&D. Revenue +34% YoY.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.note: AIP (Artificial Intelligence Platform) + military/gov AI. CapEx +132% YoY. Revenue +56% YoY.

companies.name: Siemens
companies.note: Industrial AI + Xcelerator platform + digital twin AI. Revenue EUR 75.9B.

companies.name: Salesforce
companies.note: Einstein AI + Agentforce platform + Data Cloud. FY2026 (Feb 2025-Jan 2026). AI investment through $6B R&D.

companies.name: SAP
companies.note: Joule AI assistant + Business AI. Revenue EUR 36.8B (+11% cc). AI investment through $7.15B R&D.

companies.name: Accenture
companies.note: $3B multi-year AI investment commitment + AI consulting practice. Services company, no traditional R&D line.

companies.name: Uber
companies.note: AI-powered routing, pricing, autonomous vehicle partnerships. Revenue +18% YoY.

companies.name: Adobe
companies.note: Firefly generative AI + Sensei AI. FY2025 (Dec 2024-Nov 2025). AI investment through $4.2B R&D.

companies.name: Booking Holdings
companies.note: AI Trip Planner + dynamic pricing AI. $170M strategic AI investment program in 2025.

companies.name: Infosys
companies.note: Topaz AI platform + generative AI services. IT services company, minimal capex.

companies.name: Spotify
companies.note: AI DJ + personalized recommendations + AI podcast tools. Revenue EUR 17.7B. AI investment through R&D.

companies.name: Snowflake
companies.note: Cortex AI + Arctic LLM + data lakehouse AI features. FY2025 (Feb 2024-Jan 2025). AI investment through $1.78B R&D.

companies.name: Shopify
companies.note: Shopify Magic AI + Sidekick AI assistant. Revenue +30% YoY. Very capital-light model.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.note: GPT-5 training + compute infrastructure. $110B raised (Feb 2026). $25B ARR by Feb 2026. Valuation $840B post-money.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.note: Claude model training + safety research. $30B Series G (Feb 2026) at $380B post-money. ~$20B ARR pace by Feb 2026.

companies.name: Databricks
companies.note: DBRX LLM + Mosaic AI + lakehouse platform. $5.4B ARR (+65% YoY). $134B valuation (Feb 2026).
